text: The dynamic structural changes of catalysts and their correlation with catalysis are poorly understood. In this study, we show that the light activation over a TiO2-supported Cu catalyst can control the dynamic structural changes of Cu active sites during low-temperature methanol steam reforming. Under the light activation, the thermally deactivated Cu active sites of TiO2-supported Cu catalyst can be restored from inactive Cu2O to the active metallic Cu by photoinduced charge carriers generated from TiO2. As a result, the light activated Cu/TiO2 catalyst presents a higher catalytic performance. With low intensity solar light, the optimized Cu/TiO2 catalyst shows a high H2 production rate of 1724.1 μmol g-1 min-1, which is higher than most of the reported photocatalytic and thermocatalytic processes. The in situ adjustment of Cu active sites by strong light-matter-reactant interaction reveals the possibility for real-time functionalization of catalysts.
